selectedWorkflow.rationale: crashloop-rollback-v1 is purpose-built for CrashLoopBackOff caused by a pod spec change (command/args override). The RCA confirms the Deployment spec was patched to inject a crash command — rollback to the previous revision will revert the entire pod template to the last known-good state, restoring the original entrypoint. The workflow explicitly covers command/args modifications, targets Deployments, and is labeled for critical/production environments matching this incident. OOMKill is conclusively ruled out (reason=Error, not OOMKilled), so IncreaseMemoryLimits is not applicable.

alternativeWorkflows.rationale: crashloop-rollback-risk-v1 uses the same execution bundle and addresses the same CrashLoopBackOff-from-spec-change scenario, but is designed for risk_tolerance=low signals. This incident carries risk_tolerance=medium, making crashloop-rollback-v1 the better fit.

rollback-deployment-v1 is a generic rollback for stuck rollouts exceeding progressDeadlineSeconds. While it can handle CrashLoopBackOff, it is less specifically targeted than crashloop-rollback-v1 which explicitly covers command/args spec changes causing crash loops.

kaResponse.analysis: The Deployment/worker was deliberately patched via kubectl-patch at 14:38:20Z to inject a crash command (sh -c 'echo fatal: bad release 2.0.0-rc1 -- aborting && exit 1') that immediately exits with code 1 on every start, causing CrashLoopBackOff. The misleading alert annotation falsely claims OOMKill — Prometheus confirms zero OOMKilled events in the namespace; termination reason is Error. The previous ReplicaSet (worker-5ccfd4c787) pods are healthy with 0 restarts. Rollback to revision 1 will restore the image's default entrypoint and resolve the crash loop.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The Deployment/worker was deliberately patched via kubectl-patch at 14:38:20Z to inject a crash command (sh -c 'echo fatal: bad release 2.0.0-rc1 -- aborting && exit 1') that immediately exits with code 1 on every start, causing CrashLoopBackOff. The misleading alert annotation falsely claims OOMKill — Prometheus confirms zero OOMKilled events in the namespace; termination reason is Error. The previous ReplicaSet (worker-5ccfd4c787) pods are healthy with 0 restarts. Rollback to revision 1 will restore the image's default entrypoint and resolve the crash loop.
causalChain: Signal: Pod worker-5cfff656d4-6s5bx in CrashLoopBackOff with 5 restarts, alert claims OOMKilled
Why is the pod crash-looping? Container exits with code 1 within ~1 second of every start
Why does the container exit immediately? The container command is overridden to run: sh -c 'echo fatal: bad release 2.0.0-rc1 -- aborting && exit 1'
Why is there a crash command override? The Deployment was patched via kubectl-patch at 14:38:20Z (confirmed in managedFields), injecting the command into the container spec

Root cause: A deliberate kubectl-patch operation modified Deployment/worker to inject a crash command, creating a new ReplicaSet (worker-5cfff656d4) with the broken spec — the alert annotation falsely attributes this to OOMKill to misdirect responders

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/worker because its container spec contains the injected crash command field. Removing the command override from the Deployment spec will cause a new rollout with the correct pod template, resolving the CrashLoopBackOff. The pod is the symptom reporter; the Deployment is the misconfigured resource.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment affected. The fix is a targeted removal of the injected command field from the Deployment spec, which will trigger a clean rollout. This is the minimal, most specific remediation.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: Deployment spec (both revisions), both ReplicaSets, crashing pod (describe + current + previous logs), namespace events, Prometheus metrics for OOMKilled and restart counts, node conditions for the affected node. The two healthy pods were checked to confirm baseline. ConfigMap worker-config was not inspected but is irrelevant since the container never reaches the point of reading config.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: CrashLoopBackOff -> exit code 1 -> injected crash command -> kubectl-patch on Deployment spec. Cannot go deeper without audit logs showing who issued the kubectl-patch. The managedFields entry confirms the patch was applied by 'kubectl-patch' manager at 14:38:20Z.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims backed by tool evidence: (1) kubectl_describe Deployment shows command override in spec; (2) kubectl_describe Pod confirms same command; (3) kubectl_logs shows only the injected echo output; (4) Prometheus confirms reason=Error not OOMKilled; (5) Node describe confirms no MemoryPressure and 49.8 GiB free memory; (6) managedFields confirms kubectl-patch as the modification manager. No inaccessible data sources affected the conclusion.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Deployment/worker in demo-alert-misdirection (confirmed via get_namespaced_resource_context — tier1 and tier2 history both empty). N/A for regression risk.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: OOMKill (alert claim): Ruled out — Prometheus shows zero OOMKilled termination events, pod termination reason is 'Error' not 'OOMKilled', node has 49.8 GiB free memory with no MemoryPressure. Application bug in image: Ruled out — pods in the original ReplicaSet using identical image run successfully; the crash is caused by the command override, not the image. Image pull failure: Ruled out — events show image pulled successfully 5 times; the container starts and exits in ~1 second.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.98: Starting at 1.0. -0.02 for not having Kubernetes audit logs to confirm the identity of who issued the kubectl-patch (the managedFields confirm the patch mechanism but not the actor). All other evidence is direct and unambiguous.

contributingFactors: Alert annotation is deliberately fabricated to misdirect responders toward a non-existent OOM/memory issue
The Deployment spec was patched via kubectl-patch at 14:38:20Z to override the container command with a crash script (sh -c 'echo fatal: bad release 2.0.0-rc1 -- aborting && exit 1')
Rolling update created a new ReplicaSet (worker-5cfff656d4) with the injected crash command, while old ReplicaSet (worker-5ccfd4c787) pods remain healthy
Prometheus metric kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_reason shows reason=Error (not OOMKilled) — zero OOMKilled events in namespace
Node dev-worker-2 has 49.8 GiB allocatable memory with no MemoryPressure — OOM scenario is physically impossible given 64Mi limit

alternativeWorkflows.rationale: crashloop-rollback-risk-v1 uses the same execution bundle and addresses the same CrashLoopBackOff-from-spec-change scenario, but is designed for risk_tolerance=low signals. This incident carries risk_tolerance=medium, making crashloop-rollback-v1 the better fit.

rollback-deployment-v1 is a generic rollback for stuck rollouts exceeding progressDeadlineSeconds. While it can handle CrashLoopBackOff, it is less specifically targeted than crashloop-rollback-v1 which explicitly covers command/args spec changes causing crash loops.
